title: ci: validate workflow run SHA before uploading release assets
author: skools-here

findings.description: ARGS+=("${{ inputs.release_name }}") uses an inline GitHub Actions expression inside a bash body. If release_name contains shell metacharacters (quotes, semicolons), it can break array syntax or inject commands. Fix: assign to env var RELEASE_NAME and use ARGS+=("$RELEASE_NAME").
Two commits where the second is a fixup of the first; consider squashing into one atomic commit.
eval $CMD replaced with proper bash array node push-release-assets.js "${ARGS[@]}" — eliminates command injection via string concatenation.
ASSETS=($(ls -1 ...)) replaced with find -print0 / null-delimited read loop — safe for filenames with spaces.
New SHA validation step checks each workflow run's headSha against the release tag commit SHA — supply-chain provenance improvement.
CNCF CLA signed; @illume CHANGES_REQUESTED (lowercase commit subject) resolved in updated commits.
